chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitution Petition No. D- 2132 of 2024 [Dr. Noor Hussain Chandio vs. PO Sindh and others] Hearing of case (Priority) 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For orders on CMA No.7843/2024 (S/A) 3. For hearing of main case. 06.05.2026. Mr. Sarfraz A. Akhund, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Sheeraz Fazal, Advocate for Respondent No.8. Mr. Adnan Ahmed Memon, Advocate for Respondent No.9. Syed Naveed Ahmed Shah, Deputy Attorney General. Mr. Ali Raza Baloch, Additional Advocate General Sindh *********************** O R D E R At the very outset, learned counsel for the petitioner submits that, in view of the comments filed by Respondents No.2, 3, 4, and 5, namely the Higher Education Commission (HEC), particularly para-14 thereof, it has been indicated that the petitioner may file a formal complaint before the University of Karachi under Section 6 of the HEC’s Graduate Education Policy, 2023, for redressal of his grievance against Respondent No.9 and others. In light of the availability of such an alternate remedy, learned counsel submits that he does not wish to press the instant Constitutional Petition. Consequently, the instant Constitutional Petition is dismissed as not pressed, along with all listed applications. Order accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE
